config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diprioritaskan tanpa batas, mudah sekali disalahgunakan oleh pemerintah atau mayoritas untuk menekan kelompok kecil. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat aturan yang adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan kebebasan dan martabat orang per orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dihormati terlebih dahulu lalu diselaraskan dengan kepentingan umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily that logic can be used to justify abuse by governments or majorities. I do think society has legitimate needs, especially around public safety, disease control, and basic order, but those limits have to be narrow, fair, and constantly checked. If individual rights are treated as secondary by default, then minority groups and unpopular people are the first ones to lose protection. To me, a healthy society is one that solves collective problems without treating personal liberty as disposable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling terdampak biasanya kelompok kecil atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau ketertiban umum, tetapi pembatasannya harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa diuji, bukan jadi cek kosong untuk mayoritas. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru yang kuat melindungi hak individu, lalu mencari kepentingan bersama tanpa menginjak martabat orang per orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as superior by default, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to excuse overreach, conformity, and abuse of unpopular minorities. Public health and safety do matter, but I do not trust broad claims about the "greater good" unless the limits are narrow, evidence-based, and subject to real checks. When society's interests are made the top priority in principle, individual rights stop being rights and start becoming permissions granted by whoever has power. To me, a free society is one that protects the person first and then builds the common good around that protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, dan yang paling sering kena justru orang kecil atau kelompok yang tidak punya suara. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik dan ketertiban umum, tetapi itu harus jadi pengecualian yang ketat, bukan alasan default untuk menyingkirkan hak individu. Buat saya, kepentingan bersama yang sehat justru dibangun di atas perlindungan hak orang per orang, bukan dengan menjadikannya nomor dua.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that kind of principle has too often been used to justify coercion, censorship, and discrimination against unpopular people. I do think there are real exceptions, like public health emergencies or immediate threats to safety, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. Once “society” becomes the default trump card, individual rights stop being rights and become privileges that can be taken away whenever the majority finds it convenient. To me, a healthy society protects the person first and only restricts rights when it can make a very strong case that doing so is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
